summary: Synthetic support interaction with misleading evidence, abandoned paths, conflicting observations, a hypothesis reversal, and a late root-cause reveal.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: This is becoming a real blocker for us. customer reports symptoms consistent with more than one access issue.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Okay, I can check a few things with you. When did this first start?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: the issue began after a recent operational change

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That points me toward license limit, but I want to keep this tentative. What changed right before the issue started?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: The confusing part is this: An early symptom seems to support license limit, but later evidence weakens it.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Let's test license limit first. If that fits, we should see the same behavior on a known-good example.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: We checked one example and it seemed to fit, but then another example did not. customer reports symptoms consistent with more than one access issue, but at least one comparison case behaves differently.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That weakens license limit. I am going to abandon that path for now and check browser cache.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: browser cache happened to us once before, so that sounds plausible, but the symptoms are not exactly the same.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Agreed. browser cache is possible, but the evidence is mixed. I need a comparison between a working and blocked case.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: One working case has the same visible setup. Also, an admin later mentions disabled role mapping rule.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That reverses the direction. The visible setup may not be the control point. I want to inspect the shared configuration boundary.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: I just got a note from someone else: a working example differs in one hidden configuration detail. customer has a time-sensitive rollout or business deadline and trust is degrading.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That late detail finally makes the actual path coherent. The likely root cause is: The access failure is caused by disabled role mapping rule.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: So the earlier checks were not wasted, but they were pointing at the wrong control point?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Correct. The earlier paths were plausible but abandoned after conflicting evidence. Final outcome: agent identifies disabled role mapping rule as the likely operational cause, but certainty depends on the selected resolution type
